goal: postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, f) = compose[T, U, V](left, f)
postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, g) = compose[T, U, V](left, g)
compose[T, U, V](left, f) = compose[T, U, V](left, g)
postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, f) = postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, g)
postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, f) = postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, g) = (f = g)
precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, f) = compose[T, U, V](f, right)
proof: let w0: T satisfy { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, f, w0) != compose[T, U, V](f, right, w0) }
function[T0, T1, T2](x0: T0 -> T1, x1: T1 -> T2, x2: T0) { precompose_fn[T0, T1, T2](x0, x1, x2) = compose[T0, T1, T2](x1, x0, x2) }[T, U, V](right, f, w0)

goal: precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, g) = compose[T, U, V](g, right)
proof: let w0: T satisfy { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, g, w0) != compose[T, U, V](g, right, w0) }
function[T0, T1, T2](x0: T0 -> T1, x1: T1 -> T2, x2: T0) { precompose_fn[T0, T1, T2](x0, x1, x2) = compose[T0, T1, T2](x1, x0, x2) }[T, U, V](right, g, w0)

goal: compose[T, U, V](f, right) = compose[T, U, V](g, right)
precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, f) = precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, g)
precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, f) = precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, g) = (f = g)
is_injective_fn[T -> U, T -> V](postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left))
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1) { forall(x1: T0, x2: T0) { x0(x2) != x0(x1) or x2 = x1 } = is_injective_fn[T0, T1](x0) }[T -> U, T -> V](postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left))
let w0: T -> U satisfy { exists(k0: T -> U) { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, k0) = postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, w0) and k0 != w0 } }
let w1: T -> U satisfy { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, w0) = postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, w1) and w0 != w1 }
let w2: T -> U satisfy { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, w0) = postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, w2) and w0 != w2 }
let w3: T satisfy { w2(w3) != w0(w3) }
function(x0: T -> U, x1: T -> U, x2: T) { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, x0) != postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, x1) or x0(x2) = x1(x2) }(w0, w2, w3)

goal: is_injective_fn[U -> V, T -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1) { forall(x1: T0, x2: T0) { x0(x2) != x0(x1) or x2 = x1 } = is_injective_fn[T0, T1](x0) }[U -> V, T -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))
let w0: U -> V satisfy { exists(k0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, k0) = precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w0) and k0 != w0 } }
let w1: U -> V satisfy { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w0) = precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w1) and w0 != w1 }
let w2: U -> V satisfy { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w0) = precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w2) and w0 != w2 }
let w3: U satisfy { w2(w3) != w0(w3) }
function(x0: U -> V, x1: U -> V, x2: U) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x0) != precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x1) or x0(x2) = x1(x2) }(w0, w2, w3)

goal: postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, source, x) = compose[T, U, V](left, source, x)
compose[T, U, V](left, source, x) = left(source(x))
proof: function[T0, T1, T2](x0: T1 -> T2, x1: T0 -> T1, x2: T0) { compose[T0, T1, T2](x0, x1, x2) = x0(x1(x2)) }[T, U, V](left, source, x)

goal: source(x) = compose[T, V, U](inverse_fn[U, V](left), target, x)
compose[T, V, U](inverse_fn[U, V](left), target, x) = inverse_fn[U, V](left, target(x))
proof: function[T0, T1, T2](x0: T1 -> T2, x1: T0 -> T1, x2: T0) { compose[T0, T1, T2](x0, x1, x2) = x0(x1(x2)) }[T, V, U](inverse_fn[U, V](left), target, x)

goal: postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, source, x) = target(x)
exists(k0: T -> U) { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, k0) = target }
proof: function(x0: T -> U) { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, x0) != target }(source)

goal: is_surjective_fn[T -> U, T -> V](postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left))
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T1 -> T0) { forall(x1: T0) { exists(k0: T1) { x0(k0) = x1 } } = is_surjective_fn[T1, T0](x0) }[T -> V, T -> U](postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left))
let w0: T -> V satisfy { forall(x0: T -> U) { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, x0) != w0 } }
function(x0: T -> V) { exists(k0: T -> U) { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, k0) = x0 } }(w0)
let w1: T -> U satisfy { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, w1) = w0 }
let w2(arg0: T -> V) -> w2_result: T -> U satisfy { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, w2_result) = arg0 }
function(x0: T -> V) { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, w2(x0)) = x0 }(w0)
(forall(x1: T -> U) { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, x1) != w0 } = true)
function(x0: T -> U) { postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left, x0) != w0 }(w2(w0))

goal: precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, source, x) = compose[T, U, V](source, right, x)
proof: function[T0, T1, T2](x0: T0 -> T1, x1: T1 -> T2, x2: T0) { precompose_fn[T0, T1, T2](x0, x1, x2) = compose[T0, T1, T2](x1, x0, x2) }[T, U, V](right, source, x)

goal: compose[T, U, V](source, right, x) = source(right(x))
proof: function[T0, T1, T2](x0: T1 -> T2, x1: T0 -> T1, x2: T0) { compose[T0, T1, T2](x0, x1, x2) = x0(x1(x2)) }[T, U, V](source, right, x)

goal: source(right(x)) = compose[U, T, V](target, inverse_fn[T, U](right), right(x))
compose[U, T, V](target, inverse_fn[T, U](right), right(x)) = target(inverse_fn[T, U](right, right(x)))
proof: function[T0, T1, T2](x0: T1 -> T2, x1: T0 -> T1, x2: T0) { compose[T0, T1, T2](x0, x1, x2) = x0(x1(x2)) }[U, T, V](target, inverse_fn[T, U](right), right(x))

goal: precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, source, x) = target(x)
exists(k0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, k0) = target }
proof: function(x0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x0) != target }(source)

goal: is_surjective_fn[U -> V, T -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T1 -> T0) { forall(x1: T0) { exists(k0: T1) { x0(k0) = x1 } } = is_surjective_fn[T1, T0](x0) }[T -> V, U -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))
let w0: T -> V satisfy { forall(x0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x0) != w0 } }
function(x0: T -> V) { exists(k0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, k0) = x0 } }(w0)
let w1: U -> V satisfy { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w1) = w0 }
let w2(arg0: T -> V) -> w2_result: U -> V satisfy { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w2_result) = arg0 }
function(x0: T -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w2(x0)) = x0 }(w0)
(forall(x1: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x1) != w0 } = true)
function(x0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x0) != w0 }(w2(w0))

goal: is_injective_fn[T -> U, T -> V](postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left))
is_surjective_fn[T -> U, T -> V](postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left))
is_bijection_fn[T -> U, T -> V](postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left))
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1) { (is_injective_fn[T0, T1](x0) and is_surjective_fn[T0, T1](x0)) = is_bijection_fn[T0, T1](x0) }[T -> U, T -> V](postcompose_fn[T, U, V](left))

goal: is_injective_fn[U -> V, T -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))
is_surjective_fn[U -> V, T -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))
is_bijection_fn[U -> V, T -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1) { (is_injective_fn[T0, T1](x0) and is_surjective_fn[T0, T1](x0)) = is_bijection_fn[T0, T1](x0) }[U -> V, T -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))

goal: is_bijection_fn[T, V](compose[T, U, V](f, g)) = is_bijection_fn[U, V](f)
is_bijection_fn[T, V](compose[T, U, V](f, g)) = is_bijection_fn[T, U](g)
is_injective_fn[Bijection[T, U], Bijection[T, V]](compose_bijection[T, U, V](e))
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1) { forall(x1: T0, x2: T0) { x0(x2) != x0(x1) or x2 = x1 } = is_injective_fn[T0, T1](x0) }[Bijection[T, U], Bijection[T, V]](compose_bijection[T, U, V](e))
let w0: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { exists(k0: Bijection[T, U]) { compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, k0) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w0) and k0 != w0 } }
let w1: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w0) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w1) and w0 != w1 }
let w2: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w0) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w2) and w0 != w2 }
function(x0: Bijection[T, U], x1: Bijection[T, U]) { compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, x0) != compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, x1) or x0 = x1 }(w0, w2)

goal: is_injective_fn[Bijection[U, V], Bijection[T, V]](function(x0: Bijection[U, V]) { compose_bijection[T, U, V](x0, e) })
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1) { forall(x1: T0, x2: T0) { x0(x2) != x0(x1) or x2 = x1 } = is_injective_fn[T0, T1](x0) }[Bijection[U, V], Bijection[T, V]](function(x3: Bijection[U, V]) { compose_bijection[T, U, V](x3, e) })
let w0: Bijection[U, V] satisfy { exists(k0: Bijection[U, V]) { compose_bijection[T, U, V](k0, e) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](w0, e) and k0 != w0 } }
let w1: Bijection[U, V] satisfy { compose_bijection[T, U, V](w1, e) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](w0, e) and w1 != w0 }
let w2: Bijection[U, V] satisfy { compose_bijection[T, U, V](w0, e) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](w2, e) and w0 != w2 }
let w3: Bijection[U, V] satisfy { compose_bijection[T, U, V](w3, e) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](w0, e) and w3 != w0 }
function(x0: Bijection[U, V], x1: Bijection[U, V]) { compose_bijection[T, U, V](x0, e) != compose_bijection[T, U, V](x1, e) or x0 = x1 }(w3, w0)
function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1) { forall(x1: T0, x2: T0) { x0(x2) != x0(x1) or x2 = x1 } = is_injective_fn[T0, T1](x0) }[Bijection[T, U], Bijection[U, T]](inverse_bijection[T, U])
let w0: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { exists(k0: Bijection[T, U]) { inverse_bijection[T, U](k0) = inverse_bijection[T, U](w0) and k0 != w0 } }
let w1: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { inverse_bijection[T, U](w0) = inverse_bijection[T, U](w1) and w0 != w1 }
let w2: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { inverse_bijection[T, U](w0) = inverse_bijection[T, U](w2) and w0 != w2 }
function[T0: Inhabited, T1: Inhabited](x0: Bijection[T1, T0], x1: Bijection[T1, T0]) { inverse_bijection[T1, T0](x0) = inverse_bijection[T1, T0](x1) = (x0 = x1) }[U, T](w0, w2)

goal: exists(k0: Bijection[T, U]) { inverse_bijection[T, U](k0) = h }
proof: function(x0: Bijection[T, U]) { inverse_bijection[T, U](x0) != h }(inverse_bijection[U, T](h))
function[T0, T1](x0: T1 -> T0) { forall(x1: T0) { exists(k0: T1) { x0(k0) = x1 } } = is_surjective_fn[T1, T0](x0) }[Bijection[U, T], Bijection[T, U]](inverse_bijection[T, U])
let w0: Bijection[U, T] satisfy { forall(x0: Bijection[T, U]) { inverse_bijection[T, U](x0) != w0 } }
function[T0: Inhabited, T1: Inhabited](x0: Bijection[T1, T0]) { inverse_bijection[T0, T1](inverse_bijection[T1, T0](x0)) = x0 }[T, U](w0)
(forall(x1: Bijection[T, U]) { inverse_bijection[T, U](x1) != w0 } = true)
function(x0: Bijection[T, U]) { inverse_bijection[T, U](x0) != w0 }(inverse_bijection[U, T](w0))
function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1) { (is_injective_fn[T0, T1](x0) and is_surjective_fn[T0, T1](x0)) = is_bijection_fn[T0, T1](x0) }[Bijection[T, U], Bijection[U, T]](inverse_bijection[T, U])

goal: is_injective_fn[X -> B, X -> C](postcompose_fn[X, B, C](f))
is_injective_fn[B -> X, A -> X](precompose_fn[A, B, X](g))
